[[BLOCK:srt_85]]
Speaker 2: You.

[[BLOCK:srt_86]]
Speaker 2: Okay.

[[BLOCK:srt_87]]
Speaker 3: Just look at me.

[[BLOCK:srt_88]]
Speaker 3: My next question is: How does CGU Online help students strike a balance between career development, personal life, and academic growth?

[[BLOCK:srt_89]]
Speaker 1: Most of our students are working professionals.

[[BLOCK:srt_90]]
Speaker 1: They have full-time jobs during the day.

[[BLOCK:srt_91]]
Speaker 1: And many of them—arguably most—also have families and the responsibilities that come with them.

[[BLOCK:srt_92]]
Speaker 1: So we fully understand that for adults who are juggling work and family, education has to fit into the rhythm of their lives.

[[BLOCK:srt_93]]
Speaker 1: Because the courses are delivered online, students can study on their own schedules. They might wait until the evening, after the kids are asleep, to finally sit down and complete their assignments. Or they may take advantage of the early morning hours, before the rest of the household wakes up, to get some studying done.

[[BLOCK:srt_94]]
Speaker 1: We recognize that those moments are precious, so our course design truly centers on their schedules.

[[BLOCK:srt_95]]
Speaker 3: That really is a learner-centered model.

[[BLOCK:srt_96]]
Speaker 3: Programs like AI map and Sid are also becoming increasingly popular among students in China.
